generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance IncubatorHeatbedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActuatingComponent [
    base:desc "Primary heating element controlled by Raspberry Pi for maintaining target temperature in the insulated incubator"
]

instance IncubatorFanActuator : DTDFVocab:ActuatingComponent [
    base:desc "Fan for uniform heat distribution within the styrofoam insulated incubator container"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance InternalTemperatureSensor1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Internal temperature sensor (first internal sensor) measuring thermal state of the incubator heatbed"
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTempSensorData
]

instance InternalTemperatureSensor2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Internal temperature sensor (second internal sensor) measuring thermal state within the insulated styrofoam box"
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTempSensorData
]

instance ExternalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "External temperature sensor measuring ambient temperature outside the styrofoam insulated container"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ExternalTempSensorData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance InternalTempSensorData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor1, InternalTemperatureSensor2
]

instance ExternalTempSensorData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalTemperatureSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BasicThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Basic thermal model with two free parameters representing simplified heat transfer dynamics of the incubator"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithms, StateEstimationAlgorithms
]

instance AdvancedThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Advanced thermal model with four free parameters capturing detailed thermal inertia of the heatbed for more accurate temperature predictions"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithms, StateEstimationAlgorithms
]

instance TemperatureDataStream : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time temperature data stream collected from all three sensors (2 internal + 1 external) for real-time monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTempSensorData, ExternalTempSensorData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithms, MonitoringAlgorithms
]

instance CalibrationParametersData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Calibration parameters derived from sensor data and model fitting for parameter estimation (thermal conductivity, heat loss coefficients)"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithms
]

instance ModelValidationMetrics : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Validation metrics comparing DT predictions with physical measurements (RMSE, parameter estimation error 5%)"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ValidationAlgorithms
]

// C11: Enablers
instance RaspberryPiController : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Central cyber controller integrating sensor data and executing control algorithms via embedded Raspberry Pi platform"
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlStrategyRecommendationsService, StateEstimationService
]

instance CalibrationAlgorithms : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Automated parameter tuning for models using system identification techniques (least-squares fitting) to minimize prediction errors"
    DTDFVocab:enables BasicThermalModel, AdvancedThermalModel
]

instance MonitoringAlgorithms : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Fault detection and state estimation algorithms (residual analysis, Kalman filters) for detecting sensor anomalies and reconstructing true system states"
    DTDFVocab:enables FaultDetectionService, StateEstimationService
]

instance DifferentialEquationSimulator : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulation engine implementing differential equations for thermal dynamics modeling (heat transfer, convection) to predict temperature behavior"
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictionService
]

instance StateEstimationAlgorithms : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Algorithms for reconstructing system states from noisy sensor data (Kalman filters, particle filters) to improve model accuracy"
    DTDFVocab:enables StateEstimationService
]

instance SupervisoryControllers : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Rule-based or model-predictive controllers enforcing safety/optimality constraints (e.g., ±1°C temperature target) with energy efficiency considerations"
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlStrategyRecommendationsService
]

instance VersionControlRepository : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Git repository managing model updates (refined differential equations, sensor calibration tables) for version control and reconfigurability"
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelEvolutionService
]

// C6: Services
instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring of temperature data collection and continuous validation against safety/operational thresholds"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeTemperatureMonitoringInsight, ThresholdViolationAlertAction
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of heating/fan control parameters to achieve target temperature stability with minimal energy use"
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlStrategyRecommendations, EnergyOptimizationAction
]

instance PredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Simulation-based forecasting of temperature behavior under varying conditions (load changes, sensor noise) using differential equation models"
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureSpikePredictionInsight, PreemptiveActionRecommendation
]

instance ControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Reconfigurable control via DT-driven adjustments to hardware/software parameters (heatbed power, fan speed) for environmental disruption mitigation"
    DTDFVocab:provides ActuatorAdjustmentAction, SafetyCriticalFeedbackLoop
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Advanced real-time visualizations of temperature maps, sensor data trends, and model predictions for user monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureVisualizationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeTemperatureMonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous tracking of temperature trends (1-minute intervals) across internal/external sensors for real-time system monitoring"
]

instance TemperatureSpikePredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics identifying potential temperature spikes due to high heatbed usage or fan inefficiency via calibrated models"
]

instance FaultDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Algorithmic fault detection identifying sensor anomalies or model mismatches through residual analysis"
]

instance StateEstimationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Reconstruction of true system state (e.g., internal temperature) from noisy sensor data using state estimation algorithms"
]

instance ControlStrategyRecommendations : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Suggested adjustments to actuators (fan speed, heatbed power) to meet target temperature (±1°C) with minimal energy use"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EnergyOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Optimized control parameters implementation to reduce energy consumption while maintaining temperature stability"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance PreemptiveActionRecommendation : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic actuator adjustments triggered by predictive analytics to prevent temperature deviations before they occur"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ThresholdViolationAlertAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Alerts triggered when temperature exceeds safety thresholds (40°C) for manual intervention"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ActuatorAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Autonomous actuator changes (fan/heatbed adjustments) based on DT predictions indicating suboptimal performance"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SafetyCriticalFeedbackLoop : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Emergency cooling or other safety-critical actions activated when temperature deviations are detected"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance TemperatureVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time graphical representation of temperature distribution and trends for user monitoring and decision support"
]

// C1: Environment
instance IncubatorEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains InternalTemperatureSensor1, InternalTemperatureSensor2, ExternalTemperatureSensor,
        IncubatorHeatbedActuator, IncubatorFanActuator
]

instance CalibrationEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains TemperatureDataStream, CalibrationParametersData, ModelValidationMetrics
]

instance SimulationEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains BasicThermalModel, AdvancedThermalModel, DifferentialEquationSimulator
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
  The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in the incubator DT involves **command execution** from the digital model to the physical system via the Raspberry Pi controller. Key components include:
  - **Control commands**: Adjustments to the **heatbed** and **fan** (e.g., power modulation, speed regulation) to regulate temperature.
  - **Command formats**: Likely **binary or PWM (Pulse-Width Modulation)** signals for actuators (heatbed/fan), validated by the DT’s supervisory algorithms.
  - **Feedback systems**: The DT monitors **temperature deviations** (via sensors) and triggers corrections if thresholds are exceeded, ensuring closed-loop control.
  - **Validation/authentication**: Implicitly handled by the DT’s **calibration algorithms** (Document 1) and **state estimators** (Document 1) that verify command feasibility before execution.
  - **Conflict resolution**: If the DT detects inconsistencies (e.g., sensor noise, model drift), it may **pause commands** or apply **adaptive control strategies** (e.g., fallback to predefined safety modes).
  **Execution confirmation**: Not explicitly detailed, but implied to occur via **acknowledgment signals** from the Raspberry Pi to the DT upon successful actuator engagement.
 "
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) for the incubator case study employs a **real-time synchronization** mechanism between the physical twin (PT) and its digital counterpart, leveraging a **Raspberry Pi-based control system** for data acquisition and processing. The temporal granularity is tied to the **sampling frequency of the temperature sensors** (two internal and one external), which are likely synchronized at a **low-to-mid frequency** (e.g., **1 Hz or less**) to capture dynamic thermal fluctuations. Calibration algorithms update models in near-real-time to reflect operational changes, ensuring alignment between the PT and DT. Latency requirements are implicitly tied to the **control loop** (e.g., fan/heatbed adjustments) and are optimized for **sub-second response times** to maintain predictive accuracy under varying environmental conditions. The DT’s time scale is optimized for **real-time monitoring and adaptive regulation**, with no explicit mention of ultra-low-frequency or batch-processing modes in the provided documents."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin constellation for the incubator follows a **closed-loop orchestration pattern**, integrating the following key components:
  1. **Data Layer**: Temperature sensors (internal/external) feed real-time measurements to the DT via the Raspberry Pi’s I/O interfaces.
  2. **Model Layer**: Two calibrated models (likely physics-based or empirical) are employed to simulate the thermal dynamics of the PT, updated via calibration algorithms (e.g., parameter estimation from sensor data).
  3. **Control Layer**: Supervisory controllers (e.g., PID-like regulators) and state estimators (e.g., Kalman filters) process model-data fusion to generate control signals for the PT’s actuators (fan/heatbed).
  4. **Coordination**: A **centralized control loop** synchronizes PT and DT operations, with no explicit mention of federated or multi-agent architectures. The DT’s architecture is depicted in **Figure 1** (from Document 1), where calibration and monitoring are tightly coupled to the physical system’s control infrastructure."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle for the Incubator case study spans **development, deployment, and operational phases**, with a focus on **model calibration, simulation, and real-time monitoring** tied to the Physical Twin (PT). Key stages include:

1. **Conceptual Design Phase**:
   - Abstract models (e.g., CAD-like representations) are created to capture structural aspects (e.g., styrofoam box geometry, sensor placements) but **lack dynamic behavior** (e.g., force dynamics, thermal distribution). These are later refined into **detailed physics-based models** (e.g., differential equations for temperature regulation) during the **simulation phase** (Document 1).

2. **Modeling & Simulation Phase**:
   - **Calibration algorithms** (e.g., parameter estimation via sensor data) are applied to align DT models with the PT’s cyber-physical behavior (Document 1, Fig. 1). The DT employs **two distinct models** (as implied in Document 2, Section 3) for the incubator’s CPS:
     - **Low-fidelity models** (e.g., simplified thermal dynamics) for rapid prototyping.
     - **High-fidelity models** (e.g., differential equations) for accurate prediction of temperature regulation under varying conditions (e.g., content inside the box).
   - **Transition Management**: Models evolve via iterative calibration loops, where sensor data (e.g., internal/external temperature from 2–3 sensors) updates parameters dynamically (Document 1, Fig. 1).

3. **Deployment Phase**:
   - The DT integrates with the **Physical Twin (PT)**, which includes:
     - A **Raspberry Pi** as the control system (Document 2, Fig. 2).
     - Sensors (2 internal + 1 external) for real-time data acquisition.
   - The DT’s **monitoring algorithms** (e.g., fault detectors, state estimators) operate in tandem with the PT’s actuators (fan/heatbed) to ensure **safety and optimality** (Document 1).

4. **Operational Phase**:
   - **Continuous feedback loops** between the DT and PT enable:
     - **Predictive analytics** (e.g., simulating temperature deviations due to content changes).
     - **Adaptive control** (e.g., adjusting fan/heatbed based on DT-predicted violations of target temperature).
   - **Lifecycle Governance**: The DT’s evolution is governed by **version control** (e.g., Git-based repositories for model updates, as referenced in Document 2, GitHub link). Future work (Document 2, Section 5) emphasizes **scalability** and **reconfigurability** for robustness against environmental changes.

No explicit governance framework (e.g., ISO standards) is detailed, but the lifecycle aligns with **Industry 4.0 principles** (Document 2) for CPS-DT integration.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
  **Model Fidelity Requirements:**
  The DT’s fidelity is driven by the **four-parameter model**, which outperforms the two-parameter model in predicting **thermal inertia** of the heatbed. This model is deemed more appropriate for further DT development, implying:
  - **High-fidelity requirements** for thermal dynamics (e.g., differential equation-based simulations).
  - **Parameter sensitivity**: The four parameters (vs. two) enhance model accuracy but may introduce complexity.

  **Validation and Verification Methods:**
  - **Experimental validation**: The incubator case study uses **open-source hardware/software** to empirically test model performance, comparing predictions against real-world data.
  - **Model comparison**: The four-parameter model is validated against the two-parameter model’s limitations (e.g., insufficient thermal inertia capture).
  - **Uncertainty quantification**: Implicitly addressed via **calibration algorithms** that refine model parameters iteratively using sensor data.

  **Error Detection and Correction:**
  - **Calibration loop**: Algorithms update models dynamically to minimize discrepancies between simulated and real-world behavior (e.g., via sensor feedback).
  - **Monitoring**: Post-deployment, monitors detect deviations (e.g., temperature violations) to trigger corrective actions (e.g., adjustments to the physical heatbed or control systems).

  **Quality Assurance Processes:**
  - **Iterative development**: The DT is built incrementally, with the four-parameter model replacing the basic model as a validation step.
  - **Open-source collaboration**: The accessible platform enables community-driven verification of DT techniques, ensuring reproducibility and transparency.
  "
]

 "

    base:desc "
  The **deployment infrastructure** for the incubator DT is **on-premise/edge-based**:
  - **Hosting platform**: Deployed on a **Raspberry Pi** (acting as the CPS controller) or a **local edge server** (for processing sensor data and control logic).

  **Note**: The DT’s hardware/software specs are available in the public repository [2], but specifics on scalability/availability are inferred from general DT principles (Documents 1–2).
